content: Fixed-timestep accumulator: cap the accumulator itself (or the frame-time fed into it) rather than letting update-count grow unbounded; e.g. clamp frame_time to a max (e.g. 0.25s) before adding to accumulator, and/or cap max ticks drained per frame (e.g. 5), so a stall causes visible slow-motion catch-up rather than a freeze or a jump. Canonical source: Gaffer On Games 'Fix Your Timestep!'; pygame wiki ConstantGameSpeed implements the same pattern with pygame.time.Clock.
